jokes.text: Oh... Oh What is this? A meatball? Really?
jokes.setup: Dwight discovers something unexpected in his workspace
jokes.punchline: It's a meatball - an absurd office prank
jokes.explanation: Simple physical prank setup. Dwight's indignant reaction is character-appropriate but predictable.

jokes.text: It's always more fun to mess with Dwight with an audience. That was usually Pam. So now that she's out, I had to find someone else
jokes.setup: Jim explains his pranking methodology
jokes.punchline: He needs to replace Pam as his prank audience
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jim's methodical approach to pranking and Pam's absence affecting his behavior.

jokes.text: Turns out that Stanley is quite the comedy fan
jokes.setup: Stanley as Jim's new prank audience
jokes.punchline: The reveal that grumpy Stanley enjoys watching pranks
jokes.explanation: Excellent character subversion - Stanley being a 'comedy fan' is genuinely surprising and fits the show's character depth.

jokes.text: But not everything makes him laugh. He has very specific tastes.
jokes.setup: Stanley likes comedy
jokes.punchline: But he's picky about it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Stanley's character quirk, building anticipation for what he finds funny.

jokes.text: Through a painstaking process of trial and error, I found out what he likes. And it's really weird
jokes.setup: Jim did extensive research on Stanley's comedy preferences
jokes.punchline: What Stanley finds funny is 'really weird'
jokes.explanation: Sets up anticipation well. Jim treating comedy like a science experiment is very character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Jim, come on. So juvenile
jokes.setup: Stanley is supposedly enjoying Jim's pranks
jokes.punchline: He criticizes them as juvenile while clearly being entertained
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley moment - his grumpy exterior while secretly enjoying the show. The irony is well-executed.

jokes.text: You've been meatballed
jokes.setup: Jim's prank reveal
jokes.punchline: Turning 'meatball' into a verb for pranking
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's instantly quotable. Creating a new prank verb is creative and economical.

jokes.text: Are you ready for some meatballs?
jokes.setup: The meatball prank continues
jokes.punchline: Jim turns it into a sports-style announcement
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the meatball concept, referencing 'Monday Night Football' theme.

jokes.text: This is not very clever, Jim
jokes.setup: Dwight's response to being pranked
jokes.punchline: He tries to diminish Jim's prank while clearly being flustered
jokes.explanation: Standard Dwight deflection. His attempt at dismissal while being clearly bothered is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Really, Jim? Really?
jokes.setup: Dwight finds more meatballs
jokes.punchline: His exasperated double 'really' shows mounting frustration
jokes.explanation: Predictable escalation beat. Functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: What's the haul? Thirty-two meatballs. Good day. That idiot's been feeding us for a week. We'll never have to buy meatballs again
jokes.setup: Stanley and Phyllis collecting meatballs from Jim's prank
jokes.punchline: They've been taking advantage of the prank for free food
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - the 'victims' of the prank are actually benefiting. Perfect character moment for penny-pinching office workers.

jokes.text: I'm taking an Italian class. So far, I have learned tortellini, spaghettini, linguine. Well, it's not so much a class as a restaurant. But I do Monday Wednesday, Friday from 7:00 to 9:00
jokes.setup: Kelly claims to be taking Italian classes
jokes.punchline: She's just going to a restaurant and learning pasta names
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly moment - her delusional self-improvement mixed with commitment to the bit. The pasta vocabulary is a great touch.

jokes.text: You sound really pretty today
jokes.setup: Kelly talking to someone on the phone
jokes.punchline: She compliments how someone 'sounds' pretty
jokes.explanation: Good Kelly absurdism - the illogical compliment is very her. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: Pump the brakes, Bernard
jokes.setup: Andy talking to himself about proposing
jokes.punchline: He uses his own last name to address himself
jokes.explanation: Great character quirk - Andy's tendency to talk to himself formally. The last name usage is a nice touch.

jokes.text: I haven't proposed to anyone in years
jokes.setup: Andy considering proposing
jokes.punchline: Implies he's proposed to multiple people before
jokes.explanation: Dark character humor suggesting Andy's romantic history. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Mom took the main diamond out. She thought that had more of a 'my little brother' kind of vibe to it
jokes.setup: Andy's family ring for proposing
jokes.punchline: His mom infantilizing him through jewelry modifications
jokes.explanation: Excellent Andy family dysfunction. The specific phrase 'my little brother' vibe is perfectly awkward and quotable.

jokes.text: Got a little bit of The Shining vibe, though
jokes.setup: Looking at Robert's massive house
jokes.punchline: References the creepy hotel from The Shining
jokes.explanation: Solid pop culture reference that fits the moment. Not groundbreaking but appropriate and economical.

jokes.text: Big, stupid, pure chump bait
jokes.setup: Dwight commenting on Robert's house
jokes.punchline: He calls an expensive house 'chump bait' while clearly being impressed
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight jealousy masquerading as disdain. 'Chump bait' is a great phrase that sounds like him.

jokes.text: The 1% are suffering too, people
jokes.setup: Robert can't sell his expensive house
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically appeals for sympathy for rich people during recession
jokes.explanation: Good topical humor about wealth inequality. Robert's self-awareness makes it work rather than seeming tone-deaf.

jokes.text: I bought it to be my Playboy Mansion. A temple to wine, revelry, sex, intrigue. This was hot on the heels of Eyes Wide Shut, mind you.
jokes.setup: Robert explaining his house purchase motivation
jokes.punchline: He was inspired by a controversial erotic film to buy a party house
jokes.explanation: Perfect Robert California grandiosity. The specific film reference and formal language ('temple to') is very character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Then I met my wife, she moved in, made it her own. Now she's left me and forced me to sell the place. The ultimate insult - they're calling my speakeasy lounge a rumpus room
jokes.setup: Robert's marriage ruined his party house dreams
jokes.punchline: Real estate agents are using pedestrian terminology for his sophisticated spaces
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy. Robert's pretentious language ('speakeasy lounge') vs mundane reality ('rumpus room') is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: What was the joke you thought you heard?
jokes.setup: Robert catches Jim laughing at his pain
jokes.punchline: He demands Jim explain what was funny about his misery
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment. Robert's intensity and Jim's discomfort create perfect tension. The direct confrontation is uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Kind of laughing at your own pain. Sad clown thing
jokes.setup: Jim trying to explain why he laughed
jokes.punchline: He awkwardly psychoanalyzes Robert as a 'sad clown'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy. Jim's attempt to intellectualize his inappropriate laughter makes it worse. 'Sad clown thing' is nicely awkward.

jokes.text: Oh, yes. How hilarious it is to laugh at clowns, the painted jesters of a dying circus industry
jokes.setup: Jim mentioned sad clowns
jokes.punchline: Robert turns it into overwrought social commentary
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Robert California moment. The phrase 'painted jesters of a dying circus industry' is perfectly pretentious and quotable.

jokes.text: I'm getting reports of a serious outbreak of the 'grumpies' in here
jokes.setup: Kevin addressing workplace tension
jokes.punchline: He infantilizes the mood by calling it 'grumpies'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment. His childlike vocabulary and formal 'reports' structure is exactly his character.

jokes.text: A beautiful monster cost me my 40s and my dream home
jokes.setup: Robert explaining his divorce
jokes.punchline: He calls his ex-wife a 'beautiful monster'
jokes.explanation: Great Robert poetry. 'Beautiful monster' is evocative and economical, and the specific 'my 40s' detail adds weight.

jokes.text: Well, please tell Susan we all say hi
jokes.setup: Robert is clearly bitter about his divorce
jokes.punchline: Erin cheerfully sends regards to his estranged wife
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin obliviousness. Her sweet attempt at politeness in an inappropriate moment is very character-specific.

jokes.text: Like some sort of last hurrah? Yeah. All of us in the pool saying hurrah. Maybe the last one that says hurrah is 'it.'
jokes.setup: Kevin suggests a pool party as a 'last hurrah'
jokes.punchline: He literally wants people to say 'hurrah' and turn it into a game of tag
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kevin logic. Taking the metaphor literally and turning it into a children's game is perfectly Kevin.

jokes.text: Hey, Oscar, was that you who just created a party out of thin air or was it me? That was you, Kevin. It was me
jokes.setup: Kevin wants credit for suggesting the party
jokes.punchline: He asks Oscar to confirm, then immediately answers himself
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin moment showing his need for validation and simple logic. The self-answering is appropriately childlike.

jokes.text: You going tonight, kiddo? Because I can give you a lift. Oh, I don't know Meredith, it seems like you shouldn't drive maybe ever
jokes.setup: Meredith offers Erin a ride
jokes.punchline: Erin bluntly questions Meredith's driving ability
jokes.explanation: Good character clash. Erin's innocent honesty meets Meredith's questionable lifestyle choices.

jokes.text: How do you know where I live? Andy followed you home after the Christmas party. Why? He wanted to make sure California didn't put it in you
jokes.setup: Erin asks how Meredith knows her address
jokes.punchline: Andy stalked her home to protect her from Robert's advances
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about workplace creepiness and stalking behavior. The crude phrasing is very Meredith.

jokes.text: Wow. Andy's such a weird stalker. Following me home like that when he has a girlfriend, a restraining order
jokes.setup: Erin commenting on Andy's stalking behavior
jokes.punchline: She casually mentions he has a restraining order
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke. The restraining order reveal is delivered with perfect Erin casualness.

jokes.text: Well, we'll say it's 82 and it'll be our secret
jokes.setup: Robert checking the pool temperature
jokes.punchline: He conspiratorially suggests lying about the temperature reading
jokes.explanation: Mild Robert creepiness. Making mundane temperature readings seem intimate is uncomfortable but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: Wow. Just stopping by. Got another party to go to. A wife and two kids at home party. DJ Pam Halpert is spinning some serious Radio Disney tonight.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining why he can't stay long
jokes.punchline: He frames domestic life as another party with Pam as DJ playing kids' music
jokes.explanation: Great reframing of parenthood. 'DJ Pam Halpert spinning Radio Disney' is creative and perfectly captures married life with kids.

jokes.text: I don't mean to brag but New Year's Eve, I was home by 9:00.
jokes.setup: Jim describing his party-leaving strategy
jokes.punchline: He brags about leaving New Year's Eve extremely early
jokes.explanation: Perfect dad humor. Bragging about going home early on the party night of the year is wonderfully subversive.

jokes.text: Funny how you can be surrounded by people and still feel so lonely
jokes.setup: Ryan making a deep observation at a party
jokes.punchline: He delivers pseudo-philosophical commentary in a social setting
jokes.explanation: Good Ryan pretentiousness. The cliched profundity is very him, though the joke itself is predictable.

jokes.text: How about this weekend we take that sucker to a duck pond or something? Maybe get caught in the rain. Well, I can't. I'm going skiing with Jessica. Couple of dopes on the slopes.
jokes.setup: Ryan suggesting a romantic car adventure with Andy
jokes.punchline: Andy casually mentions his girlfriend and makes a skiing pun
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment. Ryan's awkward flirting met with Andy's oblivious response and weak pun.

jokes.text: But if he was jealous once before then maybe I can make him jealous again. Just not with Robert. He told me he was a ride I wouldn't survive and I believe him.
jokes.setup: Erin planning to make Andy jealous
jokes.punchline: She rules out Robert because of his sexual threat
jokes.explanation: Dark workplace humor. The euphemistic 'ride I wouldn't survive' is both funny and genuinely concerning.

jokes.text: You know, you are just as dumb at night
jokes.setup: Angela observing someone at the party
jokes.punchline: She notes their stupidity is consistent across time periods
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela insult. The observation that stupidity is time-consistent is economical and cutting.

jokes.text: I moved them. Pants only need a chair if there's a person in them
jokes.setup: Someone asking about their moved pants
jokes.punchline: Stanley's practical logic about furniture usage
jokes.explanation: Excellent Stanley logic. The matter-of-fact delivery of absurd reasoning about pants and chairs is perfectly him.

jokes.text: You want to play you little hick?
jokes.setup: Pool confrontation brewing
jokes.punchline: Dwight's condescending challenge using 'little hick'
jokes.explanation: Standard Dwight aggression. 'Little hick' is character-appropriate but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: You enter this room a lawyer, a doctor, a teacher, a judge, but beyond it you are simply a penis a vagina, a hunger, an ache
jokes.setup: Robert giving a house tour
jokes.punchline: He describes his parlor in overly sexual, philosophical terms
jokes.explanation: Peak Robert California. The pretentious reduction of humanity to base instincts is perfectly uncomfortable and quotable.

jokes.text: Susan used it as a Pilates studio
jokes.setup: Robert's grandiose description of his parlor
jokes.punchline: His ex-wife turned it into something mundane and healthy
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Robert's pretension. The contrast between 'hunger and ache' and Pilates is expertly executed.

jokes.text: With ammunition like this, we are in for quite a night you and me. And Gabey makes three
jokes.setup: Toby talking about wine as ammunition
jokes.punchline: Gabe arrives and Toby makes a nursery rhyme reference
jokes.explanation: Mild Toby attempt at being social and clever. The nursery rhyme reference is gentle but not particularly sharp.

jokes.text: Why would you choose me? Because I'm mighty? Because I'm the manliest man in the office? I'll do it!
jokes.setup: Erin asking Dwight to help make Andy jealous
jokes.punchline: Dwight's ego immediately takes over and he agrees enthusiastically
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight vanity. His immediate assumption that he was chosen for his manliness is exactly right for his character.

jokes.text: I pictured myself here every night eating a leg of mutton, the juices dripping down my bare chest, wiping my fingers on the walls
jokes.setup: Robert describing his fantasy life in the house
jokes.punchline: He imagined eating like a medieval barbarian, wiping food on walls
jokes.explanation: Incredible Robert California moment. The specific 'leg of mutton' and 'wiping fingers on walls' details are perfectly grotesque and memorable.

jokes.text: Then I met the vegan
jokes.setup: Robert's barbaric eating fantasies
jokes.punchline: His wife's veganism ended those dreams
jokes.explanation: Masterful economy. Four words completely deflate his elaborate fantasy. The timing and understatement are perfect.

jokes.text: Toby, you are playing a dangerous game
jokes.setup: Toby getting into Robert's wine and philosophy
jokes.punchline: Robert warns him about getting too deep into his world
jokes.explanation: Good Robert menace. The ominous warning about wine appreciation is appropriately uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I guess I'm through the gateway now though, right?
jokes.setup: Toby referencing Robert's earlier 'gateway' metaphor
jokes.punchline: He nervously asks if he's crossed into Robert's hedonistic world
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing Toby absorbed in Robert's philosophy. His nervousness about crossing the threshold is endearing.

jokes.text: I've been working out but the problem is, I've been building muscle underneath and that top layer hasn't burned off yet. Awkward stage
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining why he won't swim
jokes.punchline: His misunderstanding of how fitness works
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kevin logic about fitness. The idea that muscle builds 'underneath' fat like geological layers is perfectly Kevin.

jokes.text: Now take a chip and crush it into my face and rub the grease around. Do it. Yeah, rub it in. Yeah. That's so good
jokes.setup: Erin trying to make Andy jealous through food play
jokes.punchline: She gets uncomfortably sensual with chip grease
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy. Erin's attempt at seduction through junk food is appropriately awkward and desperate.

jokes.text: Andy's not even looking. Eating is a dead end. Damn it.
jokes.setup: Erin's food seduction attempt
jokes.punchline: She matter-of-factly declares eating unsuccessful as a jealousy tactic
jokes.explanation: Great Erin deadpan. 'Eating is a dead end' as relationship strategy is wonderfully clinical and absurd.

jokes.text: I know. I could just bang you right now
jokes.setup: Erin complimenting their teamwork in the pool
jokes.punchline: Dwight misinterprets and responds with crude directness
jokes.explanation: Typical Dwight inappropriate response. His complete misreading of the situation is character-appropriate crude humor.

jokes.text: I had two bears sewn together to make this king size. Total waste of two bears
jokes.setup: Robert showing off his bed
jokes.punchline: He regrets the extravagance now that he's alone
jokes.explanation: Excellent Robert excess. The specific detail of bear-skin bedding and his regret makes it both absurd and poignant.

jokes.text: To both these bears
jokes.setup: Robert's regret about the two bears
jokes.punchline: They toast the deceased bears
jokes.explanation: Nice immediate callback. The solemnity of toasting dead bears is appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: When I put in the screening room I bought three movies. Caligula, Last Tango in Paris and Emmanuelle 2. The last two movies I actually watched in here were Marley & Me and On Golden Pond.
jokes.setup: Robert's erotic film collection for his screening room
jokes.punchline: He ended up watching wholesome family movies instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Robert's hedonistic plans and domestic reality. The specific film choices are well-selected opposites.

jokes.text: Kelly, that's a crazy ring you found. Yeah. Thanks, I'm really glad that I found it
jokes.setup: Angela commenting on the ring Kelly is wearing
jokes.punchline: Kelly is obliviously happy about wearing someone's lost engagement ring
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast. Kelly's obliviousness to the ring's significance versus Angela's awareness.

jokes.text: Ring of a failed marriage might have some sinister energy, right? Am I just being silly? I don't think you're being silly. Oh, God. You know what? I can just sell it and put the money in the party fund. Oh. Then another woman will get it, we can't allow that. We have to destroy it
jokes.setup: Angela warns about the ring's bad luck
jokes.punchline: They decide they must destroy it like the One Ring
jokes.explanation: Good escalation into fantasy territory. The Lord of the Rings parallel works well with their dramatic personalities.

jokes.text: Does Darryl not swim? That's racist. I don't know but I would say by looking at him, no, Darryl does not swim
jokes.setup: Kevin asking about Darryl's swimming
jokes.punchline: Angela calls out the racism then immediately continues the stereotype
jokes.explanation: Good character hypocrisy moment. Angela's awareness of racism followed immediately by perpetuating it is perfectly written.

jokes.text: You've broken up your last couple, you evil ring. Do it. We're in the pool. Shove it, Angela.
jokes.setup: Kelly trying to destroy the cursed ring
jokes.punchline: She throws it in the pool while Angela objects
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the ring subplot. Kelly's dramatic 'evil ring' address and Angela dismissal work well together.

jokes.text: But boy, that Erin, she sure is a ripe little tiger, isn't she? Meow! And to think I always thought of her as a second Meredith
jokes.setup: Gabe commenting on Erin at the party
jokes.punchline: His creepy sexual observations with animal sounds
jokes.explanation: Standard Gabe creepiness. The animal metaphors and meowing are appropriately uncomfortable but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: It's monogamy for my hog and me
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his commitment to Jessica
jokes.punchline: He makes a crude rhyme about faithfulness
jokes.explanation: Typical Andy crude humor. The rhyme is mildly clever but the content is juvenile.

jokes.text: So then you won't mind if tonight I just go crazy on her? Just go nuts... With sex
jokes.setup: Gabe testing Andy's feelings about Erin
jokes.punchline: His awkward, explicit sexual proposition
jokes.explanation: Standard Gabe awkwardness. The explicit clarification is uncomfortable but predictable for his character.

jokes.text: You're an idiot
jokes.setup: Gabe's crude proposition about Erin
jokes.punchline: Andy's simple, direct response
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction. The economy is perfect, though it's not particularly creative.

jokes.text: How did you know it was mine? The Bernard family seal, duh. Duh
jokes.setup: Erin returning Andy's ring
jokes.punchline: Andy's family has a seal on the ring and he says 'duh' twice
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his privilege (family seal) and his awkward social skills (double 'duh').

jokes.text: Andy's confused. That's not what I was hoping for. But it's not so bad either
jokes.setup: Erin's talking head about Andy's reaction
jokes.punchline: She finds optimism in his confusion about their relationship
jokes.explanation: Good Erin optimism. Her ability to find hope in confusion is very character-appropriate and endearing.

jokes.text: Robert, I want you to know I'm here to rock with you as late as you want. And that goes double for me. I'll stay even later than you'd like
jokes.setup: Toby pledging to stay late at the party
jokes.punchline: Gabe one-ups him by offering to overstay his welcome
jokes.explanation: Good character competition. Both trying to impress Robert but Gabe's offer to overstay is perfectly awkward.

jokes.text: Toby, I am Bacchus god of wine. And I am Bacchus' friend
jokes.setup: Robert declaring himself a Roman god
jokes.punchline: Toby settles for being the god's friend
jokes.explanation: Great character contrast. Robert's grandiosity meets Toby's modest ambitions perfectly. The mythological reference fits Robert.

jokes.text: And there's my talking point
jokes.setup: Something notable happens at the party
jokes.punchline: Jim references his earlier strategy about creating memorable moments
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to Jim's earlier talking-head about party strategy. Shows his self-awareness and planning.

jokes.text: It's not a party if you don't do something that scares you
jokes.setup: Robert watching people enjoy his pool
jokes.punchline: His philosophical take on what makes a real party
jokes.explanation: Good Robert wisdom. The observation about fear and fun is both profound and pretentious in the perfect Robert way.
